data.problem: A local farm is selling baskets of apples and baskets of peaches. On the first day of sales, they sold 30 baskets of apples and 10 baskets of peaches for a total of $550. They took in $615 for 30 baskets of apples and 15 baskets of peaches on the second day. Find the price of each type of item.
data.solution_steps:
\begin{aligned}
\text{Let } a &\text{ = price of basket of apples} \\ 
\text{Let } p &\text{ = price of basket of peaches} \\ 
 \\ 
30a + 10p &= 550 \\ 
30a + 15p &= 615 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Subtract equations:} & \\ 
5p &= 65 \\ 
p &= 13 \\ 
 \\ 
\text{Substitute back:} & \\ 
30a + 10(13) &= 550 \\ 
30a + 130 &= 550 \\ 
30a &= 420 \\ 
a &= 14
\end{aligned}
